text.title: Stage-Indexed Tactics and Quick Wins
text.summary: Conversion tactics organized by funnel stage (ToF, MoF, BoF) with aggregate lift bands, plus six high-impact quick wins with concrete benchmarks.

section.title: Top of Funnel (Awareness)
section.p: Goal: get users to engage with your product or content. Levers: headline clarity, page speed, ad targeting precision, social proof, value proposition specificity.

list.item: Clearer headlines (one concrete benefit, not feature list): +10-30% engagement.
Page load under 3 seconds: +5-20% conversion (each second after 2s costs ~7%).
Better ad targeting (narrow audience, specific message): +15-40% qualified traffic.
Social proof above fold (user count, logos, testimonials): +5-15% trust signal.
Value prop clarity (what, for whom, why different): +10-25% bounce reduction.

section.title: Middle of Funnel (Consideration)
section.p: Goal: get users to take action (signup, demo, trial). Levers: form field count, progress indicators, trust badges, CTA design, friction removal.

list.item: Reduce form fields to minimum required: +10-25% completion (B2B SaaS typical; B2C lead gen can reach +100%).
Add progress indicators for multi-step flows: +5-15% completion.
Trust badges (security, certifications, guarantees) near CTA: +8-18% conversion.
Action-oriented CTA in high-contrast color: +5-20% click rate.
Remove friction (no credit card required, guest checkout): +15-40% signup rate.

section.title: Bottom of Funnel (Conversion)
section.p: Goal: complete purchase or activation. Levers: guest checkout, payment method variety, error clarity, urgency (must be honest), risk reversal.

list.item: Guest checkout (no forced account creation): +15-30% checkout completion.
Multiple payment options (card, Apple Pay, PayPal): +10-20% checkout completion.
Clear, specific error messages that tell users exactly how to fix the issue: +5-15% form recovery.
Money-back guarantee visible at decision point: +8-15% conversion.
Exit-intent popups (desktop only — no mouse-leave on mobile): +5-12% recovery.

rule.statement: Run these six quick-win tactics first — they are high-impact, low-effort, and have the strongest benchmark evidence. Always validate on mobile and desktop separately; a win on desktop can be a loss on mobile.
rule.rationale: Quick wins build organizational confidence in CRO programs and fund investment in larger experiments. Skipping directly to complex tests wastes momentum.

list.item: Form fields: remove all non-essential fields; gather additional info after signup via progressive profiling. B2B target: 3 fields or fewer for initial signup.
Progress bar: add step indicator to any multi-step process. +15% completion rate.
Button contrast: if CTA is not in top-3 clicked elements on heatmap, increase contrast. High-contrast green/orange: +50% click rate vs low-contrast gray.
Social proof placement: near CTA (+15% conversion) and above fold (+20% trust). User count + logos + 1 testimonial with photo and name is the minimum viable set.
Page speed: target LCP under 2s. Each second over 2s costs 7% conversion. Quick fixes: WebP images, lazy load, CDN, minify CSS/JS.
Copy simplification: replace jargon with one concrete outcome statement. "Ship projects faster with AI planning" outperforms "enterprise-grade, cloud-native workflow optimization solution".
